paper.title: Can LLM Agents Respond to Disasters? Benchmarking Heterogeneous Geospatial Reasoning in Emergency Operations
paper.comment: DORA stress-tests LLM agents on real-world disaster operations that demand comprehensive orchestration of 108 specialized tools over heterogeneous geospatial data

checks: pdfinfo identifies a readable PDF 1.7 document with the complete title, ten-author byline, and 13 pages.
The layout-preserving extraction is non-empty and readable from the title, abstract, and methods through the conclusion, acknowledgments, and complete references on manuscript page 13.
The extraction preserves the reported 515-task, 45-event, five-dimension, 108-tool, configured-system, trajectory-length, ablation, failure-mode, and conclusion sections needed by the queued review.
The immutable arXiv source archive passed gzip/tar inventory checks. Its 16 regular files include the complete TeX manuscript, bibliography, class file, and 13 figure/image assets.
The immutable v1 abstract page records the submission timestamp, title, author list, abstract, comment, cs.AI category, and no withdrawal or retraction notice.
warnings: The arXiv Atom API returned HTTP 429 during acquisition, so immutable-v1 metadata was verified against the arXiv abstract page, PDF first page, and source archive rather than the Atom response.
Layout-preserving extraction separates figure labels and may hyphenate words across visual columns; use the PDF for page-precise review claims.

official_release_search.paper_link_evidence: The immutable v1 manuscript and complete TeX source contain no DORA project, code, dataset, tool, task, trajectory, annotation, prompt, output, result, or supplemental-release URL. The conclusion says the authors 'will release DORA with all data, tools, and evaluation protocols,' which is a future-release statement rather than a verifiable release locator.
searches: Exact benchmark name and exact paper title with GitHub, code, and dataset terms
Exact arXiv ID with GitHub-focused web search
Current public GitHub repository inventory for first author Junjue-Wang
First-author and second-author publication pages, including their links attached to the DORA entry
official_release_search.search_result_boundary: Search results found the immutable arXiv record, author publication pages, third-party paper listings, and earlier author-owned disaster/geospatial projects, but no author-owned repository or dataset identifying DORA or arXiv 2605.11633. The first-author publication page links only the paper for DORA, and the current Junjue-Wang GitHub inventory has no DORA repository. This records absence at acquisition time, not proof that no release exists or will later appear.
missing_for_reproduction: the 515 task records and 45-event sampling manifest
expert identities or authority scopes, authoring records, verification records, adjudications, and accepted alternative trajectories
the 108-tool MCP library with exact versions, schemas, implementations, dependencies, and runtime data
event imagery, social-vector layers, valid-time and license/provenance manifests, and frozen environment state
the approximately 3,500 gold tool-call steps and replay records
agent prompts, harness and scaffold implementations, exact model/service snapshots, seeds, retries, invalid/tool-failure records, and costs
run-level trajectories, artifacts, reports, grader outputs, result tables, and analysis code

acquisition_scope: Downloaded the real immutable arXiv v1 PDF and source archive, extracted layout-preserving full text, verified readable coverage through the final page, and searched for an author-owned empirical release. This is source acquisition and extraction verification, not a deep review, reproduction, endorsement, or evidence of operational disaster response, professional validity, safe evacuation, capability, production fitness, or readiness.
review_handoff: Use the immutable v1 PDF/text and TeX source for the queued deep review. Preserve the release ceiling: the manuscript is locally verifiable, but the task/tool/data/trajectory/annotation/prompt/output/result package promised for future release was not publicly verifiable at acquisition time. Recheck author-owned release availability before final review if material.
local_storage_note: The PDF and arXiv source archive are ignored local evidence; the extracted full text, paper-index entry, and this provenance record are versioned. Re-fetch ignored artifacts from the immutable URLs and verify SHA-256 when absent locally.
